summary.intent: Fix a plugin manifest validation error and ensure the optimize-anything Claude Code plugin fully adheres to marketplace/plugin creation specifications before pushing.
summary.outcome: The plugin.json manifest was corrected (author object, removed inline commands/skills arrays) and enriched with recommended marketplace metadata (repository, homepage, license). A full audit confirmed all 7 commands and 2 skills have valid YAML frontmatter and the directory layout is correct.

repo: plugin.json defines identity/metadata only; component registration happens via auto-discovery of commands/ and skills/ directories
commands/ and skills/ live at the plugin root, not inside .claude-plugin/
The skill name in skills/<name>/SKILL.md frontmatter should match the directory name (kebab-case)

code.finding: author must be an object {name, email?, url?} not a plain string; commands and skills fields must not be inline arrays of component definitions — omit them entirely to use auto-discovery

workflow: Claude Code plugin schema: component metadata (name, description) belongs in each file's YAML frontmatter, not in the root manifest
marketplace.json plugins[].source of './' correctly self-references the local plugin
Recommended plugin.json fields for marketplace discoverability: repository, homepage, license

friction: Manifest validation error message ('author: Invalid input: expected object, received string') was cryptic about the required object shape
Schema distinction between manifest-level metadata and component-level frontmatter registration was not obvious, requiring doc review to clarify
open_items: CI manifest linting and frontmatter validation tooling was suggested but not implemented
Plugin not yet pushed/validated against the live marketplace installer
